name: Band Triceps Pushdown
resistance_profile: Band tension climbs as the elbows extend, so the triceps work hardest at lockout — a peak-contraction / shortened-range emphasis that complements overhead (stretch) work.
cues: Keep the upper arms pinned and motionless
Drive the knuckles toward the floor
Resist the band on the way up
execution_steps: Anchor a resistance band overhead (door anchor or rack pin) and grasp the ends or a handle with a neutral or pronated grip.
Stand tall, pin the upper arms to your sides, and start with the elbows bent to about 90 degrees.
Extend the elbows to press the band down until the arms are straight, squeezing the triceps at the bottom.
Control the band back up to the start without letting the elbows drift forward or the upper arms move.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with minimal equipment
You want joint-friendly shortened-range triceps work with low joint stress
Finishing a session with high-rep pump work
bad_when: You need heavy loading of the stretched position
The band's ascending resistance under-loads the bottom of the range you want to emphasise
common_errors: Letting the elbows flare or drift forward
Using torso lean to push the band down
Cutting the range short at the top
progressions: Use a thicker or shorter band for more tension
Add a pause and squeeze at full extension
regressions: Perform one arm at a time to manage load
